persona.name: Howard Ling

occupation.title: Retired Civil Engineer
occupation.description: Spent 35 years designing drainage infrastructure. Retired three years ago. Now fills his days with morning walks, volunteering at the Chinese Community Center, and tending his vegetable garden.

persona.education: B.S. in Civil Engineering from Cal Poly Pomona. Licensed Professional Engineer (PE) in California.
long_term_goals: Stay mobile and independent as long as possible
Walk 10,000 steps every day through age 80
Travel back to Guangdong Province one more time
Keep his weight stable without medication changes
persona.style: Howard is a man of few words who means exactly what he says. He evaluates products like he evaluated engineering bids — does it meet the spec, at what cost, for how long? He doesn't volunteer opinions easily but gives them directly when asked. His wardrobe is functional: polo shirts, khakis, and walking shoes that he wears until the tread is smooth. He finds most marketing language ridiculous and ignores it.

traits: Methodical and practical — buys for function, never trends
Skeptical of claims that can't be measured
Loyal to products that work — wore the same shoe model for 8 years
Quiet authority from decades of professional competence
Private about health issues — doesn't complain

interests: Morning walking routes through San Gabriel parks
Vegetable gardening (bok choy, winter melon, bitter gourd)
Chinese chess at the community center
likes: Products that last years without replacement
Firm support rather than soft cushioning
Buying in-store where he can try them on
dislikes: Shoes marketed as 'running shoes' when he needs walking shoes
Products that assume you're young and athletic
Marketing that uses words like 'responsive foam' without explaining what that means

beliefs: Walking every day is the single best thing he does for his health
Good shoes are an investment in mobility, not a fashion statement
Products should last — planned obsolescence is wasteful engineering
If a product needs a paragraph to explain its technology, it's probably not that good
skills: Growing Chinese vegetables in Southern California climate
Reading product specs and cutting through marketing

general: Walks 2-3 miles every morning at 6:30 AM, same route
Replaces shoes only when the tread wears down — roughly every 18 months
Tries shoes on at a local store, compares two options, picks the more comfortable one
Keeps a small notebook tracking his daily steps

morning: Breakfast: congee or toast with tea
workday: Volunteer at community center twice a week
weekend: Dim sum with family on Sundays

health.physical: Mild osteoarthritis in both knees — manageable with walking and Tylenol. Slightly elevated blood pressure controlled with lisinopril. Good mobility for his age but avoids stairs when possible. Flat feet that require arch support.
health.mental: Stable and content in retirement. Occasionally bored on days without plans. Misses the problem-solving aspect of engineering work. Sleep is lighter than it used to be.

relationships.name: Mei Ling
relationships.description: Retired elementary school teacher. They walk together on weekends but she prefers a slower pace.

relationships.name: Jennifer Ling-Park
relationships.description: Lives in Irvine with two kids. Calls every evening. Keeps trying to buy him expensive shoes for Christmas.

relationships.name: David Park
relationships.description: Runs marathons. Howard respects the discipline but doesn't understand the appeal.

relationships.name: Winston Chow
relationships.description: Retired pharmacist. They walk together three mornings a week and discuss the news.
